description:
Background: The current adapter parity epic covers VSCode/Cursor/OpenCode but not Codex Native, which is a first-class history source for daemon-first local collection.

Scope:
- Add Codex Native adapter collector/parser with deterministic checkpoint and replay-safe IDs.
- Support available local artifacts such as shell snapshots, embeddings metadata, and future transcript/history files without over-trusting any single source.
- Integrate with shared adapter taxonomy, redaction, and trust-boundary rules.
- Add malformed/partial artifact handling and deterministic fixture coverage.

Non-goals:
- No daemon supervisor orchestration in this issue.
- No company sync semantics in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue should deliver the adapter contract only. Collector orchestration and CLI onboarding should remain in the daemon epic.

acceptance_criteria: Deterministic evaluation cases cover irrelevant memories, synergy pairs, delayed outcomes, and safety regressions, with explicit thresholds that catch noisy or overconfident attribution.
description:
Background: Attribution systems fail when they confidently reward noise, miss synergy, or cannot distinguish useful signal from irrelevant context.

Scope:
- Build a deterministic calibration suite with negative controls, irrelevant-memory cases, paired-synergy cases, delayed outcomes, and safety-regression cases.
- Add pass/fail thresholds for false positives, confidence inflation, and unstable scoring under replay.
- Ensure the suite is CI-friendly and bounded.

Non-goals:
- No production dashboarding in this issue.
- No ranking integration in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns validation of the scorer, not the scorer itself or contract plumbing.
notes: Added deterministic calibration coverage in apps/api/test/core.test.ts for irrelevant memories, synergy-like sparse evidence, provisional delayed outcomes, and safety-regressed rules.
title: [P4-E10] Add attribution calibration suite with negative controls

acceptance_criteria: Policy or decision record defines minimal trace fields, pseudonymization rules, retention bounds, same-tenant join rules, and disable or kill-switch requirements for attribution traces.
description:
Background: Fine-grained usage tracing creates a privacy and governance burden that must be constrained before broad rollout.

Scope:
- Define the minimum trace fields required for attribution.
- Specify hashing or pseudonymization rules, retention windows, and same-tenant-only join rules.
- Define per-space or per-tenant disable and kill-switch behavior.
- Document operator expectations for auditability and deletion or expiration.

Non-goals:
- No scorer implementation in this issue.
- No dashboard work in this issue.

Parallelization note: This is a policy and governance slice and should not be blocked on scorer implementation details.
notes: Drafted proposed ADR in docs/adr/0010-usage-trace-privacy-retention-tenancy.md and added runbook reference in docs/runbooks/phase1-phase2-backend-delivery-runbook.md.
title: [P4-E11] Define privacy, retention, and tenant-isolation rules for usage traces

acceptance_criteria: A read-only report or dashboard shows attributed lift or harm alongside confidence and support counts, marks all output advisory-only, and links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.
description:
Background: Operators need an uncertainty-first view of attribution data before the project should trust it in any automated path.

Scope:
- Publish a report or dashboard view for attributed lift or harm, confidence, sample size, and support counts.
- Clearly label all outputs as advisory-only while the system is in shadow mode.
- Surface top helpful and harmful memories with links back to lineage and evaluation artifacts.
- Keep the surface read-only.

Non-goals:
- No mutation controls in this issue.
- No policy definition in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is reporting-only and should consume existing scorer outputs rather than reshaping them.
notes:
Added read-only  output in apps/api/src/core.ts with helpful/harmful/pending sections plus lineage refs; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.
Added read-only attribution_report output in apps/api/src/core.ts with helpful/harmful/pending sections plus lineage refs; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.
title: [P4-E12] Publish attribution report with uncertainty-first advisory UX

acceptance_criteria: Attribution may only influence bounded ranking heuristics behind per-space kill switches, monitoring compares attributed vs baseline behavior, and there is no direct path to promote, demote, or delete memory.
description:
Background: If attribution is ever allowed to influence runtime behavior, it should start with tightly bounded ranking nudges under explicit kill-switch control, never direct memory mutation.

Scope:
- Define and implement an advisory-only integration path from attribution outputs into retrieval ranking heuristics.
- Require per-space or per-tenant kill switches and easy rollback.
- Prevent attribution from directly promoting, demoting, or deleting memory.
- Add monitoring to compare ranked-with-attribution vs baseline behavior.

Non-goals:
- No direct promote or demote authority.
- No irreversible memory changes.

Parallelization note: This issue is the only runtime integration slice and must stay narrowly bounded to ranking nudges and rollback controls.
notes:
Added per-space  kill switch and bounded advisory ranking nudges in context responses with baseline-vs-ranked observability; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts and apps/api/test/server.test.ts.
Added per-space attribution_ranking_policy kill switch and bounded advisory ranking nudges in context responses with baseline-vs-ranked observability; covered in apps/api/test/core.test.ts and apps/api/test/server.test.ts.
title: [P4-E13] Gate attribution to advisory-only ranking nudges behind kill switch

acceptance_criteria: Decision record defines evidence tiers, freezes pack_id/usage_id semantics, documents advisory-only launch, and specifies which signals may or may not drive ranking, promotion, demotion, or deletion.
description:
Background: UMS already has replay-gated evaluation, explainability, and lineage, but attribution will be risky unless the project freezes what counts as evidence and what automation is allowed.

Scope:
- Define canonical pack_id, usage_id, and outcome-linkage semantics.
- Define evidence tiers: observational, deterministic replay/ablation, and randomized canary.
- Define which tiers may influence ranking, promotion, demotion, and deletion.
- Freeze an advisory-only launch posture with default-off and kill-switch language.

Non-goals:
- No scoring implementation in this issue.
- No direct mutation authority for attribution.

Parallelization note: This issue is intentionally self-contained and should produce the contract and safety envelope that other tasks can target without redesigning the roadmap.
notes: Drafted proposed ADR in docs/adr/0009-tiered-credit-assignment-safety-envelope.md and added runbook reference in docs/runbooks/phase1-phase2-backend-delivery-runbook.md.
title: [P4-E6] Freeze tiered credit-assignment contract and safety envelope

acceptance_criteria: Context responses emit stable pack_id and usage_id fields across supported surfaces, replay tests prove determinism, and payload budgets remain bounded or explicitly documented.
description:
Background: Credit assignment needs deterministic trace identifiers at context-generation time so downstream outcomes can reference what was actually surfaced.

Scope:
- Extend context surfaces to emit stable pack_id and per-item usage_id values.
- Thread the identifiers through API, CLI, and MCP contracts without leaking tenant boundaries.
- Add deterministic replay tests to ensure repeated runs emit the same ids for the same inputs.
- Keep payload growth explicitly bounded and documented.

Non-goals:
- No outcome persistence in this issue.
- No scoring or ranking logic in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns context-side identifiers only; it should not absorb outcome linkage, scoring, or dashboard work.
notes: Implemented deterministic packId/usageId emission in apps/api/src/core.ts and added contract coverage in apps/api/test/core.test.ts plus apps/api/test/server.test.ts.
title: [P4-E7] Add deterministic pack_id and usage_id emission to context surfaces

acceptance_criteria: Outcome contracts store pack_id and usage_id lineage with replay-safe same-tenant persistence, delayed-outcome semantics are documented if needed, and tests cover replay and isolation guarantees.
description:
Background: Attribution is impossible if outcomes cannot point back to the traced memory pack and the items that were actually available to the agent.

Scope:
- Extend outcome persistence contracts to accept pack_id, referenced usage_ids, and bounded artifact, test, and tool references.
- Preserve idempotent replay-safe writes and same-tenant lineage constraints.
- Define provisional vs settled outcome timestamps if the model needs delayed resolution.
- Add tests for replay safety, tenant isolation, and lineage completeness.

Non-goals:
- No attribution scorer in this issue.
- No UI or reporting work in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue is outcome-side only; it should not change context ranking or scoring behavior.
notes: Extended outcome linkage with packId, usedUsageIds, bounded tool/test/artifact refs, and provisional/settled timestamps in apps/api/src/core.ts; added replay/isolation coverage in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.
title: [P4-E8] Persist outcome linkage for traced memory usage

acceptance_criteria: Scorer computes estimated helpful or harmful contribution with explicit confidence and sample-size fields, degrades gracefully on sparse data, and has no direct path to automatic memory mutation.
description:
Background: The safest first version of attribution is shadow-only: compute estimated lift and harm, but do not let it directly mutate active memory.

Scope:
- Implement a shadow attribution scorer that consumes traced context and outcome data.
- Output uncertainty-first fields such as effect size, confidence, sample size, support thresholds, and reason codes.
- Return low-confidence or no-op results for sparse or ambiguous data instead of forcing a score.
- Keep the scorer detached from automatic promote, demote, or delete paths.

Non-goals:
- No direct ranking mutation in this issue.
- No calibration corpus in this issue.

Parallelization note: This issue owns the scorer contract and implementation only; policy, dashboards, and integration stay elsewhere.
notes:
Implemented read-only  scoring in apps/api/src/core.ts with uncertainty-first fields, replay lineage, advisory-only guarantees, and deterministic tests in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.
Implemented read-only shadow_attribution scoring in apps/api/src/core.ts with uncertainty-first fields, replay lineage, advisory-only guarantees, and deterministic tests in apps/api/test/core.test.ts.
title: [P4-E9] Build shadow-only attribution scorer with uncertainty fields

description: Background: repo quality gates now require Bun-native test execution only. Current scripts and suites still rely on tsx --test, node:test imports, and Node+TSX process spawns. Scope: migrate package test scripts to bun test, replace node:test with @effect-native/bun-test/Bun hooks, remove tsx-based test execution and Bun-incompatible spawn helpers, and keep Effect v4 compatibility. Non-goals: no Vitest, no node:test compatibility layer. Acceptance: package test scripts use bun test; no node:test imports remain; relevant spawned test helpers execute via Bun; full test suites remain green under Bun.
title: Hard-cut migrate test stack to bun:test and Bun runtime
